chunk_text: Cache invalidation is one of the two hard problems in computer science. Naming things is the other.

expected_claims.text: Cache invalidation is a hard problem in computer science.

chunk_text: Use a TTL on cached entries so stale values expire automatically after a bounded interval.

expected_claims.text: TTLs cause cached entries to expire after a bounded interval.

chunk_text: Write-through caching persists each write to the backing store before updating the cache.

expected_claims.text: Write-through caching persists writes before updating the cache.

chunk_text: Eventual consistency means readers may observe stale data until replication completes.

expected_claims.text: Readers may observe stale data under eventual consistency.

chunk_text: Avoid thundering herds by adding jitter to cache expiry times.

expected_claims.text: Jitter on cache expiry reduces thundering herds.

chunk_text: Do not serve user-specific responses from a shared CDN cache without a Vary header.

expected_claims.text: Shared CDN caches need Vary headers for user-specific responses.
